_confidence_levels.firered-anchor: Vanilla FireRed address from the pokefirered decomp / community RAM maps. NOT confirmed for Unbound — verify against a live read before trusting.
_confidence_levels.unbound-verified: Confirmed against Unbound specifically (community GameShark code, or a live diff-scanner result that unambiguously matches a known value).
_confidence_levels.candidate: Found via diff-scanner from ONE observed action. Plausible but not cross-verified — could be coincidental or part of an unrelated adjacent field. Needs a second, differently-shaped test before trusting.

pointers.name: gSaveBlock1Ptr
pointers.note: LE u32 pointing into WRAM; money, player position, flags, vars live inside this struct

party.name: gPlayerParty
party.note2: Confirmed on real hardware 2026-07-22: decoded Lv/HP/stats matched the actual in-game party exactly.
party.note: 6 x 100-byte Gen3 mon structs. Level/HP/stats at offsets 0x54-0x63 are UNENCRYPTED and directly readable for verification.

anchors.name: playerName
anchors.note: Read as all zeros on real Unbound hardware 2026-07-22 — likely shifted, needs re-discovery via diff scanner.

anchors.name: rivalName
anchors.note: Confirmed 2026-07-22: decodes to "Ryan", matches the player's real rival name.

anchors.name: cheatItemSlot
anchors.note: Target of Unbound item GameShark codes (e.g. 0x028A=Psychic Memory, 0x0250=Flying Gem). Likely a give-item/bag-add slot.

anchors.name: repelStepCount
anchors.note: Confirmed 2026-07-22: using a Max Repel changed 0x00 -> 0xFA. 0xFA = 250 decimal, exactly the step count a Max Repel grants — strong, non-coincidental match. Try a regular Repel (should write 100/0x64) or Super Repel (200/0xC8) to double-confirm, and watch it count down while walking.

anchors.name: battleStateFlag
anchors.note: Changed 0x00 -> 0x40 on entering a wild battle (before attacking), diff-scanner result from a single test. Hypothesis: a battle type/state flag byte. To confirm: check the value across a trainer battle, a double battle, and after the battle ends (should return to 0x00) - if it's consistently the same shape per battle type, it's real.

anchors.name: fieldCounterNearRepel
anchors.note: Changed 0x04 -> 0x00 on entering a wild battle, diff-scanner result from a single test. Sits exactly 4 bytes after repelStepCount, so it may be an adjacent field in the same small struct rather than something battle-related. To confirm: watch it while walking with no repel active (does it count steps or encounters?), and see if it resets on other triggers besides battle entry.
